subject: Computer Networking
title: Ethernet: The LAN Standard
description: A 60-second lesson on Ethernet: The LAN Standard.

hook.text: In 1973, Bob Metcalfe sketched a way to connect computers in an office with a shared cable. He called it Ethernet.

buildup.text: Ethernet used a thick coaxial cable. All computers listened on the same wire and took turns transmitting.

discovery.text: CSMA/CD handled collisions: listen before sending, detect conflicts, wait a random time, then retry.

twist.text: Ethernet outlasted every competitor — Token Ring, FDDI, ATM. Simplicity and low cost won the market.

climax.text: Modern Ethernet runs at 400 Gbps over fiber. The protocol evolved but the name and principles endure.

punchline.text: Ethernet won the LAN war through simplicity.
